description: Insular Southeast Asian Marker - diagnostic for Austronesian speaking populations of the Philippines and Indonesia.
Mainland Southeast Asian Marker - informative for Tai-Kadai and Austroasiatic speaking populations.
Tibeto-Burman Border Marker - diagnostic for populations along the Himalayas and Indo-Burma border.
ALDH2 Alcohol Sensitivity Variant - globally unique to East Asian populations.
Southeast Asian Informative Marker - highly prevalent in Austroasiatic and Austronesian speaking populations.
East Asian Core Marker - highly diagnostic for Sinitic and Japonic populations.
High-Altitude Adaptation Marker - informative for Tibetan and Central Asian populations.
SLC24A5 variant. Used here to distinguish between the 'pure' African and the admixed Malagasy profiles.

Han Chinese Ancestry Marker - highly prevalent in major East Asian populations.
Tibetan Ancestry Marker - informative for Himalayan and high-altitude East Asian populations.
EAS/AMR Tie-Breaker - Highly prevalent in Siberia and East Asia, sharply reduced in the Americas.
EAS/AMR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for East Asian coastal populations, effectively absent in Native Americans.
EAS/OCE Tie-Breaker - Common across Southeast Asia, drops sharply in unadmixed Melanesian/Papuan groups.
EAS/SAS Tie-Breaker - Common in Tibeto-Burman populations, filters out false East Asian noise in South Asia.
EAS/AFR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for Austronesian expansion markers, filtering out African overlap.
EAS/EUR Tie-Breaker - Highly specific to Eastern Eurasian Steppe populations.
East Asian Alcohol Flush Marker - highly informative for Japanese and Han Chinese subpopulations.
EDAR Gene Marker - diagnostic for East Asian and Native American populations.
EDAR Tie-Breaker - Highly diagnostic for East Asian and Native American ancestry.
ABCC11 Earwax Type Marker - Diagnostic for East Asian and Native American ancestry (Dry earwax).
ADH1B Alcohol Metabolism Marker - Highly informative for East Asian ancestry.
ALDH2 Alcohol Flush Marker - Highly specific to East Asian populations.
ABCC11 Earwax/Body Odor Marker - Diagnostic for East Asian and Native American ancestry.
Northeast Asian Diagnostic Marker - highly informative for populations of the North China Plain and Korea.
South Chinese Informative Marker - highlights the genetic distinction between Northern and Southern Han populations.
Himalayan/High Altitude Adaption Marker - highly informative for Tibetan and High Altitude Asian populations.
EDAR V370A variant common in East Asian populations.
Seldin 128 Ancestry Informative Marker, informative for East Asian populations such as Han Chinese, Japanese, and Korean groups.

Defining marker for Haplogroup C2, common in Mongols, Kazakhs, and indigenous Siberians.
Associated with alcohol flushing reaction in East Asian populations.
Associated with rapid breakdown of alcohol, highly prevalent in East Asian populations.
A variant found at higher frequencies in East Asian populations.
A variant in the CYP1A1 gene found predominantly in East Asian populations.
Determines dry earwax and reduced body odor, highly prevalent in East Asian populations.
Defining marker for Haplogroup O, the most common Y-DNA haplogroup in East and Southeast Asia.
Defining marker for Haplogroup O2 (formerly O3), the dominant paternal lineage in China.
